title: eval_exists_test vacuously true with no args
description: eval_exists_test iterates over forms and returns true when the loop body never executes (no forms to check). A malformed script 'if exists { fileinto "Bug"; }' compiles without error (no validation catches missing args) and at eval time the exists test returns true, executing the then-branch. The RFC requires the header-names argument, so exists with no args is invalid, but neither the compiler nor the evaluator rejects it. The evaluator should return false (or the compiler should reject it) for exists with no string arguments. Trigger: Sieve script containing 'exists' with no header-name argument. Bad behavior: condition silently evaluates as true.
